title: Monitoring the victim
description: Even a stable state can turn for the worse fast — keep watching until rescuers arrive.
steps: Keep continuously monitoring the victim until rescuers actually arrive, even if everything looked stable half an hour ago.
Remember that some dangerous conditions — internal bleeding, for example — can't be judged by eye, so keep re-checking breathing and consciousness.
Be ready for another decline even after successful CPR — regaining consciousness doesn't guarantee the victim will stay stable.

Remember that theory only works alongside practice — in-person first-aid courses (ideally outdoor-focused ones) build the muscle memory that kicks in automatically when there's no time left to recall the instructions under stress.
